name: Coordinated Inauthentic Behavior
description: Detection, conceptualisation and longitudinal study of coordinated link sharing and inauthentic network activity on social media, the methodological core of the PI's work and the foundation of the CooRnet/CooRTweet lineage.

name: Information Disorder and Disinformation Narratives
description: Mis-, dis- and malinformation in hybrid media systems, including problematic health content, hyperpartisan news and the propagation of false or misleading narratives across platforms and national contexts.

name: Platform Governance and Research Data Access
description: Algorithmic curation, content moderation and visibility policies (e.g. Meta's political content reduction), together with the shifting landscape of social media APIs, DSA-era research access and the post-CrowdTangle ecosystem.

name: Elections and Political Communication on Social Media
description: Empirical studies of electoral campaigns on digital platforms — Italian, German, Brazilian and European — covering partisanship, polarisation, political advertising and parliamentarians' online visibility.

name: MINE — Mapping Italian News
description: The PI's institutional research umbrella since 2017: mapping Italian news media coverage, partisan attention and Facebook/Instagram interaction patterns around insular and cross-partisan sources.

name: Computational Methods and LLMs for Communication Research
description: Methodological work on large language models, embeddings, text classification pipelines and digital trace analysis for political discourse and media studies — a growing axis aligned with the new 'IA Generativa e Media' course.

name: Generative AI and Media
description: Studies of generative AI as both an object (synthetic visual persuasion, AI-driven manipulation) and a tool for journalism, fact-checking and media analysis — anchored in the vera.ai project and the PI's teaching agenda.

name: Social TV and Audience Engagement
description: Long-running line on second-screen practices, TV-Twitter interactions and networked audience participation — foundational work that continues to anchor the PI's view of hybrid media systems.

name: Digital Methods and Open Research Tools
description: Development, validation and critical reflection on tools, datasets and workflows for social media research — including CooRnet, Facebook ad-based survey recruitment, and ethical/interdisciplinary debates in computational social science.

name: Polarization in Hybrid Media Systems
description: Theoretical and empirical work on how the interplay between social media and traditional media shapes political polarization, insularity and the amplification of extremes.
